decision_points: IF new regulatory requirement identified THEN update ContextAnalysis within 30 days
IF stakeholder need impacts QMS scope THEN include in InterestedPartyRegister
IF internal issue affects process interactions THEN revise ProcessMap
rules: All four inputs (strategic direction, internal issues, external issues, stakeholder needs, regulatory requirements) must be documented before ContextAnalysis is approved
Context review frequency must be at least annually
QMSScope must explicitly list all exclusions and boundaries
exceptions: If no regulatory requirements exist, record explicit statement of absence with date and approver
If organization is startup with <10 employees, stakeholder register may be limited to top 5 parties
data_requirements: strategic_direction: string from leadership meeting minutes
internal_issue: object with id, description, impact_score (1-5)
external_issue: object with id, source, likelihood (0-1)
stakeholder_need: object with stakeholder_id, need_text, priority (1-3)
regulatory_requirement: object with jurisdiction, clause, effective_date
triggers: customer or certification body request received
success_criteria: QMSScope, ProcessMap, InterestedPartyRegister and ContextAnalysis all generated with non-null values
failure_modes: Missing input leads to incomplete ContextAnalysis (detect by null fields)
Stakeholder register omits key regulatory bodies causing audit non-conformance
ProcessMap not updated after reorganization causing interaction errors in ISO9001-5 and ISO9001-6

agent_spec.decision_logic: On triggers (annual date, org change, new reg, request) validate all four inputs per rules; apply decision_points to update artifacts within SLA; flag missing data or low coverage scores
escalation_rules: null fields in ContextAnalysis after 30 days
new regulatory requirement not processed within 30 days

sop:
# SOP — Context of the Organization
**Process ID:** ISO9001-4
**Framework:** ISO 9001:2015 | **Domain:** ISO 9001
**Generated:** 2026-06-10

## Purpose
Understanding the organization and its context, interested parties, scope of QMS and the QMS itself including processes and their interactions

## Triggers
- scheduled annual review date reached
- major organizational change event logged
- new regulatory requirement published
- customer or certification body request received

## Inputs Required
- strategic direction
- internal issues
- external issues
- stakeholder needs
- regulatory requirements

## Process Steps
1. IF new regulatory requirement identified THEN update ContextAnalysis within 30 days
2. IF stakeholder need impacts QMS scope THEN include in InterestedPartyRegister
3. IF internal issue affects process interactions THEN revise ProcessMap

## Expected Outputs
- QMS scope
- process map
- interested party register
- context analysis

## Business Rules
- All four inputs (strategic direction, internal issues, external issues, stakeholder needs, regulatory requirements) must be documented before ContextAnalysis is approved
- Context review frequency must be at least annually
- QMSScope must explicitly list all exclusions and boundaries

## Exception Handling
- If no regulatory requirements exist, record explicit statement of absence with date and approver
- If organization is startup with <10 employees, stakeholder register may be limited to top 5 parties

## Success Criteria
- QMSScope, ProcessMap, InterestedPartyRegister and ContextAnalysis all generated with non-null values
- scope_completeness >= 0.95
- stakeholder_coverage >= 0.90
- context_review_frequency <= 365 days

## Compliance Requirements
- ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4
- GDPR context analysis
- regulatory landscape
test_cases: Test 1: description of happy path test
deployment_notes: Agent ready for deployment. Compliance: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 4, GDPR context analysis, regulatory landscape
